epics.name: Gift-card data accuracy
epics.goal: Every published gift-card row is verified, correctly typed, honestly dated and audited.

epics.name: Compound gift-card campaigns
epics.goal: Multi-offer source pages are represented as true sub-offers, not flattened.

epics.name: Gift-card products and acceptance
epics.goal: Product records, merchant acceptance and MCC knowledge power the detail pages.

epics.name: Product-specific promotion rules
epics.goal: Denominations, limits and caps are structured and drive honest worked examples.

epics.name: Membership and ongoing catalogues
epics.goal: Member programmes are modelled as programmes, not fake time-boxed offers.

epics.name: Ingestion and source adapters
epics.goal: The pipeline fetches respectfully, fails loudly, recovers cleanly and can grow sources.

epics.name: Admin review and editorial workflow
epics.goal: Reviewers see diffs, duplicates and context; every decision is fast, audited and reasoned.

epics.name: Stack engine
epics.goal: Stack recommendations never claim savings or compatibility the data cannot support.

epics.name: Public UX
epics.goal: Gift-card surfaces are accessible, honest in every state, and discoverable.

epics.name: Monitoring and operations
epics.goal: Pipeline state is glanceable, alerting is real, and every risky action has a runbook.

epics.name: Security and trust
epics.goal: Trust boundaries are mechanically verified, not just designed.

epics.name: Database and migrations
epics.goal: Schema, types, constraints and the publication RPC are tested and drift-proof.

epics.name: Testing and QA
epics.goal: Real-shaped fixtures, DST/degraded/a11y coverage, and CI that runs everything.

epics.name: Developer experience
epics.goal: The rituals that keep this repo safe are one-command mechanical, not tribal.

epics.name: Product growth and future capability
epics.goal: Evidence-backed growth, sequenced strictly after trust and correctness.

tickets.title: Re-verify and set expiry dates on the 7 published GCDB rows with null expiry
tickets.problem: 7 of 13 published gift-card offers have expiry_date=null because the GCDB extractor found no end date; weekly supermarket/points promos always end, so these rows will silently go stale while remaining public.
tickets.whyItMatters: Expired-but-listed offers are the single fastest way to lose user trust; the UI's honest 'No end date listed' fallback contains the harm but does not fix the data.
tickets.currentBehaviour: Rows gc-apple-big-w, gc-apple-coles, gc-luxury-escapes-…-coles, gc-restaurant-choice-…-coles, gc-tcn-baby-…-wool, gc-uber-…-giftz-co, gc-amazon-airbnb-…-qanta all show expiry_date=null and confidence='needs-verification' (prod-verified 2026-07-12).
tickets.desiredOutcome: Each row re-verified at its cited gcdb.com.au offer page; expiry set, or the offer explicitly marked ongoing (DS-008), or unpublished when the promo has lapsed.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero published gift-card rows with expiry_date null unless explicitly reviewed as ongoing
Every touched row has last_checked_at refreshed and an audit_log row from the admin edit UI
Corrections doc updated with per-row outcome
testRequirements: npm run test:giftcards (no code change expected; guard against regressions)
tickets.manualVerification: Open each gcdb.com.au offer URL on the day of editing; screenshot or note the stated end date before saving.
tickets.rollback: audit_log preserves prior values; individual admin edits are reversible.
tickets.outOfScope: Schema changes (ongoing flag is DS-008); the two mis-typed legacy rows (DS-003).
tickets.commitMessage: DS-001: Re-verify and set expiry dates on the 7 published GCDB rows with null expiry

tickets.title: ADR: compound-campaign representation (relational sub-offers vs JSON-first)
tickets.problem: One GCDB source page can describe several sub-offers (the live 33-brand Amazon row proves it); the schema forces one candidate/offer per raw item, so mixed mechanics, caps and expiry states get flattened or dropped.
tickets.whyItMatters: Every Epic B ticket hangs off this decision; choosing wrong forces a second migration later.
tickets.currentBehaviour: gift_card_offer_candidates and gift_card_offers are strictly 1:1 with a raw item; included_product_ids (022) is the only multi-item hook.
tickets.desiredOutcome: A written ADR in docs/DEALSTACK-DECISIONS.md choosing between (a) child sub-offer tables keyed by stable sub-offer keys, or (b) JSON sub-offer arrays inside the candidate first, migrating later — with dedupe, change-detection, approval-RPC and UI implications addressed, and the user's sign-off recorded.
evidence: Prod row gc-amazon-ultimate-… (33 brands, one value)
acceptanceCriteria: ADR entry with context/decision/consequences/files
Stable sub-offer key scheme specified (survives feed re-ordering and re-parses)
User has explicitly approved the direction
tickets.manualVerification: Walk the live Amazon campaign and the TCN 4-card page through the proposed model on paper.
tickets.commitMessage: DS-012: ADR: compound-campaign representation (relational sub-offers vs JSON-first)

tickets.title: Migration: compound sub-offer schema per the ADR

tickets.desiredOutcome: Additive migration implementing the DS-012 decision: sub-offer storage with stable keys, mechanics fields, per-sub-offer dates/caps, linkage to campaign/raw item; approve RPC extended; RLS default-deny preserved; manifest + types in the same commit.
acceptanceCriteria: Idempotent, additive, in-file rollback notes
RLS: no new public read paths beyond published offers
schema-manifest + regenerated types in the same commit
approve RPC handles sub-offer approval atomically
tickets.manualVerification: Apply to prod only after explicit approval; information_schema probe + offers-hash check afterwards.
tickets.commitMessage: DS-013: Migration: compound sub-offer schema per the ADR

tickets.title: classifyChange: detect sub-offer added/removed/changed within a campaign

tickets.currentBehaviour: lib/giftcards/classifyChange.ts single-offer diff vocabulary (cosmetic/material/expiry-extension/…/source-removed).
tickets.desiredOutcome: Diff operates per stable sub-offer key: added → new candidate; removed → source-removed candidate for that sub-offer; changed → existing classification per sub-offer.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture pair (campaign v1/v2) produces exactly the per-sub-offer classifications expected
Unchanged sub-offers produce no candidates (idempotency preserved)

tickets.title: Test-enforce response-size and content-type limits on the GCDB fetch path
tickets.problem: urlPolicy documents bounded response bodies and the route reports non-XML as blocked, but the gift-card fetch path lacks focused tests proving a 100MB body or text/html content-type is rejected before parsing.
tickets.whyItMatters: The fetch boundary is the pipeline's only network surface; its failure modes deserve explicit regression tests, not inherited assumptions.
tickets.currentBehaviour: lib/security/urlPolicy.ts bounds exist; tests/giftcards/giftCardIngestRoute.test.ts covers gates, not body limits.
tickets.desiredOutcome: Unit tests (DI fetch) asserting: oversized body → blocked run; wrong content-type → blocked; gcdb.com.au host allowlist enforced; redirect >3 hops rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: All four boundary cases covered and passing
Any gap found is fixed in the same ticket (bounded scope: fetch path only)
tickets.commitMessage: DS-042: Test-enforce response-size and content-type limits on the GCDB fetch path

tickets.title: Bounded retry/backoff for transient ingest fetch failures
tickets.problem: One transient network blip fails the whole every-other-day ingest run — the next attempt is ~40h later. The OzBargain monitor has backoff (lib/monitor/backoff.ts); the gift-card ingest does not.
tickets.whyItMatters: A 2-day data gap from a 2-second blip is a poor trade; retry-once-with-jitter fixes most of it.
tickets.currentBehaviour: Single fetch attempt in the ingest route; run finalised error via runGuarded.
tickets.desiredOutcome: Up to 2 in-run retries with short bounded backoff for network-class failures only (never for blocked/challenge responses); retries recorded in run metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Transient-then-success fixture completes ok with retry count recorded
Blocked/challenge responses never retried (unit-tested)
runGuarded invariants preserved
tickets.commitMessage: DS-043: Bounded retry/backoff for transient ingest fetch failures

tickets.desiredOutcome: Streak counter on the source row; after N (default 3) consecutive non-skip errors the source auto-disables (enabled=false) with an audit row and a prominent admin banner; re-enable is manual.

tickets.title: Secret inventory and rotation runbook
tickets.problem: Five secret surfaces exist (Vercel env, GitHub Actions secrets, Supabase service key, CRON_SECRET shared across three consumers, optional webhook URL) with no rotation procedure — rotating CRON_SECRET without a written order breaks health probes and the ingest trigger simultaneously; the handoff §M documents the Vercel empty-pull trap but not rotation.
tickets.whyItMatters: Rotation happens under pressure (suspected leak); pressure plus no runbook equals extended outage or skipped rotation.
tickets.currentBehaviour: No rotation doc; consumers of CRON_SECRET: Vercel env + 2 workflows + external scheduler docs.
tickets.desiredOutcome: docs/security-rotation-runbook.md: inventory table (secret, holders, consumers, blast radius), per-secret rotation order (create-new → update consumers → verify → retire), verification commands, and the empty-pull caveat.
acceptanceCriteria: Every secret in .env.example + Actions has an inventory row and rotation order
Dry-read: an operator can rotate CRON_SECRET with zero probe downtime by following it
tickets.commitMessage: DS-084: Secret inventory and rotation runbook

tickets.currentBehaviour: Per-route bearer checks (verify exact duplication level at implementation); unset-secret→503 contract shared informally.

acceptanceCriteria: All five routes import the helper; grep shows zero inline secret comparisons

tickets.title: Offer-quality scoring for ranking (design-first, after trust foundations)
tickets.problem: The 'recommended' sort on /gift-cards has no documented quality model; as offer volume grows (recurring ingestion + more sources), ranking by effective saving alone will surface high-value-but-stale or low-confidence offers above fresh confirmed ones.
tickets.whyItMatters: Ranking IS an editorial claim; it should be a documented formula, not an accident of sort order — but it only matters at volume, so this is deliberately last.
tickets.currentBehaviour: lib/giftcards/publicQuery.ts 'recommended' sort implementation (read it as the first step); confidence/staleness/value fields all exist as inputs.
tickets.desiredOutcome: A design note: scoring inputs (effective saving, confidence, freshness, expiry proximity, evidence completeness), the formula, tie-breaks, and how the score is explained to users (transparency requirement); implementation ticket cut only after approval.
evidence: Trust-first ordering requirement (user instruction: growth must not outrank trust)
acceptanceCriteria: Design note with worked examples on current prod rows; user approval before any implementation ticket is created
tickets.commitMessage: DS-108: Offer-quality scoring for ranking (design-first, after trust foundations)

iterations.name: Iteration 01 — Production data truth
iterations.objective: Every published gift-card row re-verified at source: expiries set, mechanics corrected, the duplicate resolved, near-expiry rows decided.
prerequisites: Row-level user approval per handoff §O q1
manualChecks: Each row verified at its cited source on edit day
iterations.stopCondition: All 13 published rows verified/corrected/unpublished; strict smoke green; corrections doc statuses updated.
iterations.rollback: audit_log per-row reversal via admin edit UI.

iterations.name: Iteration 02 — Queue clearance and doc truth
iterations.objective: Clear the 15-candidate review backlog, capture format at review, and make the repo's state docs stop lying.
prerequisites: IT-01 outcomes known (duplicate decision affects queue review)
iterations.stopCondition: 0 candidates in review_status='new' (or explicit defers); stale-doc claims fixed; /phase skill carries the doc-refresh step.
iterations.rollback: Approvals reversible via unpublish; doc changes git-revertible.

iterations.name: Iteration 03 — CI, validation and fixture hardening
iterations.objective: Close the CI coverage gap, make the full gate one command, fail fast on wrong Node, pin DST behaviour, and capture the real feed fixture.
manualChecks: One wrong-Node script attempt fails instructively
iterations.stopCondition: CI runs all 5 suites; validate:all green on HEAD; real-feed fixture committed.

iterations.name: Iteration 04 — Ongoing-state migration
iterations.objective: Persist the reviewed 'ongoing' state, constrain published rows to dated-or-ongoing, and flag violations in data-health.
prerequisites: Explicit user approval to apply the migration
iterations.stopCondition: Migration applied+verified, types regenerated, constraint VALIDATEd, data-health flags live.
iterations.rollback: Documented drop-column/drop-constraint path in the migration file.

iterations.name: Iteration 05 — Security probes and hygiene
iterations.objective: Convert the trust boundaries from believed to weekly-verified: RLS probe, RPC grant audit, unified bearer auth, log redaction, dependency gate, rotation runbook, CSP decision.
prerequisites: Schema-drift workflow secrets confirmed (DS-078 helps but not blocking for local runs)
manualChecks: CSP violation-report review before any flip
iterations.stopCondition: Probes green against prod; all five bearer routes on the shared helper; CSP decision recorded (flip itself needs approval).
iterations.rollback: Probes read-only; CSP flip has a one-line header rollback.

iterations.name: Iteration 06 — Ingestion hardening
iterations.objective: Make the dormant pipeline fail loudly and recover cleanly before it ever runs recurringly: anomaly detection, retries, streak auto-pause, hostile-input fixtures, boundary tests.
iterations.stopCondition: All hardening DI-tested; DS-044 streak column migration reviewed (apply needs approval).
iterations.rollback: Behaviour additive; migration per its notes.

iterations.name: Iteration 07 — Ops visibility
iterations.objective: One glanceable admin surface and externally-probed health for the gift-card pipeline, plus the runbook and emergency stop.
manualChecks: Health endpoints curled locally in fixture states
iterations.stopCondition: Status card + health signals + runbook + emergency stop shipped; workflow probes them.

iterations.name: Iteration 08 — Admin review scaling
iterations.objective: Prepare the review queue for recurring volume: change diffs, in-queue dedupe, required rejection reasons, bulk expiry tools, keyboard ergonomics.
iterations.stopCondition: All six shipped with tests; review of a synthetic 20-candidate queue is mouse-optional.

iterations.name: Iteration 09 — Stack-engine truth
iterations.objective: Stack output never contradicts detail pages or claims unobtainable savings: two-stage stackability, gates, limits, points/cash separation, uncertainty reasons.
manualChecks: Three offer/store pairs compared across surfaces
iterations.stopCondition: Cross-surface consistency test green; limit and separation invariants pinned.

iterations.name: Iteration 10 — Public polish and accessibility
iterations.objective: Bring the newest public surface to the site's launch bar: structured data, OG, a11y audit+automation, honest empty states, stale badges.
iterations.stopCondition: axe gate green; audit checklist complete; JSON-LD validated.

iterations.name: Iteration 11 — Public UX depth
iterations.objective: Mobile ergonomics, cross-seller comparison, prod-shaped visual harness and degraded-state proof.
iterations.stopCondition: Mobile drawer + comparison live; degraded e2e green; screenshot command documented.

iterations.name: Iteration 12 — Compound-campaign foundation
iterations.objective: Decide and build the sub-offer substrate: ADR, schema migration, reprocessing path, RPC test harness.
iterations.stopCondition: ADR approved; migration applied+verified; reprocess DI-tested; RPC contract tests green.
iterations.rollback: Migration rollback notes; ADR revisable before apply.

iterations.name: Iteration 13 — Multi-candidate ingestion
iterations.objective: Extraction, change detection and review UI operate per sub-offer.
iterations.stopCondition: Compound fixture round-trips with stable keys; idempotency re-proven; split UI audited.

iterations.name: Iteration 14 — New mechanics and compound completion
iterations.objective: Fixed-dollar, credit and fee-waiver mechanics end-to-end; gates extracted; the live compound row split.
manualChecks: Sub-offer review of the Amazon campaign at source
iterations.stopCondition: Mechanics tested end-to-end; no published row represents more than one mechanic/value/date set.
iterations.rollback: Mechanic migrations additive; split reversible via audit history.

iterations.name: Iteration 15 — Product and acceptance foundation
iterations.objective: Product CRUD plus the first evidence-backed product, linkage and acceptance data for TCN/Ultimate/Apple.
manualChecks: Every acceptance row's evidence URL opened and read
iterations.stopCondition: Products live with citations; 13 offers linked or noted; acceptance sections render.

iterations.name: Iteration 16 — Product rules and detail depth
iterations.objective: Denominations, structured limits, cap-aware worked examples, channel notes, staleness and logistics rendering.
manualChecks: TCN worked example matches its cap maths by hand
iterations.stopCondition: Worked examples cap-and-denomination aware; migrations applied with approval; staleness visible.

iterations.name: Iteration 17 — Recurring ingestion activation
iterations.objective: The controlled path to always-on: gate-safe test script, retention, ops confirmation, index, then the human enable per runbook.
prerequisites: Explicit user approval to enable (handoff §K Phase 8)
manualChecks: Controlled test run with guaranteed re-closure
iterations.stopCondition: Gates enabled per runbook; two consecutive healthy scheduled runs observed; queue reviewed after each.
iterations.rollback: Emergency stop (DS-077) / any single gate closes everything.

iterations.name: Iteration 18 — Programme catalogue foundation
iterations.objective: Model member programmes honestly: ADR, schema, public catalogue UI, migrate NRMA/RACV, staleness discipline.
prerequisites: User decision on programme scope (handoff §O q4)
manualChecks: NRMA/RACV rates verified at source on migration day
iterations.stopCondition: Programmes live; no published offer row fakes an ongoing programme.
iterations.rollback: Offer rows restorable from audit history; migration notes.

iterations.name: Iteration 19 — Community input and audit tooling
iterations.objective: Users can report problems; audits and post-merge verification become scripts; acceptance search ships.
manualChecks: Audit script output vs known findings
iterations.stopCondition: Report flow live (migration approved); audit script reproduces §J ground truth.

iterations.name: Iteration 20 — Future research and scale decisions
iterations.objective: Evidence-gathering and design-first tickets that gate later growth; no implementation without sign-off.
manualChecks: Each research memo cites primary sources
iterations.stopCondition: Every memo/ADR delivered with a user decision recorded; implementation tickets cut separately.

milestones.name: Milestone 1 — Accurate gift-card production data
milestones.mustInclude: Migration activation (DS-008), expiry correction (DS-001), wrong-type correction (DS-003), duplicate handling (DS-004), compound-offer containment (DS-005/DS-021 interim).
milestones.exitCriteria: Every published row verified and constraint-protected; CI covers all suites; docs truthful.

milestones.name: Milestone 2 — Rich gift-card intelligence
milestones.mustInclude: Product rules (DS-030/031/034), acceptance/MCC knowledge (DS-026), detail completeness (DS-027/028/032/033), stack integration (DS-058..DS-063).
milestones.exitCriteria: Detail pages answer the eight buyer questions from populated data; compound campaigns truthful; stack and detail never disagree.

milestones.name: Milestone 3 — Safe automated operation
milestones.mustInclude: Ingestion hardening (DS-041..DS-047), monitoring (DS-071..DS-075), recurring cron enablement (IT-17), failure recovery (DS-044/DS-077 + runbook DS-076).
milestones.exitCriteria: Recurring ingestion live with two observed healthy runs, weekly security probes green, alerting proven.

milestones.name: Milestone 4 — Scale and growth
milestones.mustInclude: Programme catalogues (IT-18), additional-source groundwork (DS-048/DS-104), contributor workflows (DS-103), personalisation strictly after trust foundations (DS-105 design-gated).
milestones.exitCriteria: Growth features shipped only where their trust prerequisites are met; every speculative item has a recorded decision.
